paper_statement: Under Assumptions 5.3 and 5.6, ALF has vanishing long-term state-decoding error when delta_epsilon tends to zero and epsilon/delta_epsilon tends to zero. For delta_epsilon=lambda/log(1/epsilon), lambda in (0,xi), the limsup error is bounded by kappa epsilon log(1/epsilon)+o(epsilon log(1/epsilon)) and has the same dominated rate as the Bayes-optimal decoder.
quantifiers: all nearly-deterministic finite-state HMMs satisfying Definition 5.1 and Assumptions 5.3 and 5.6
required_checks: implement ALF Equation 28, not a generic perturbed-transition filter
implement exact LOF/MAP as the Bayes-optimal comparator
check the paper's exact two-state model and lambda=0.7 schedule
use deterministic seeds and binomial confidence intervals
cross-check scalar and full-vector recurrences independently
verdict_rule: Finite Monte Carlo evidence alone is BLOCKED for the universal asymptotic theorem. VERIFIED requires a proof-level checker covering the stated assumptions and limits; FALSIFIED requires a counterexample satisfying every assumption.
